title: ADR 0005 money-consolidation slipped a cycle; v0.7.0 added fresh drift (LedgerExplorer)
content: Scheduled review 2026-05-29: ADR 0005 (consolidate money formatting onto src/lib/money kernel + adopt formatMoney app-wide) is STILL Proposed/unexecuted one week after drafting. Re-affirmed, not re-opened. Ground truth: formatMoney still 0 real consumers; src/lib/format.ts still USD-hardcoded/no roundMoney/no fail-loud; WealthScoreCard.tsx:195 inline $…M toFixed third path persists. KEY DRIFT this cycle: v0.7.0 refactored TransactionsCard into a thin wrapper delegating to a NEW client component LedgerExplorer.tsx — whose money render (line 222) was wired straight to the un-normalized formatSignedCurrency. So a brand-new UI surface was immediately attached to the wrong formatter = exactly the regression Task 5's guardrail prevents. This is why an un-adopted ADR rots: every new feature widens the gap. Also: v0.7.0 added src/lib/data/ (data-layer boundary), establishing a precedent for shared non-feature libs under src/lib/ — strengthens ADR 0005's shared-kernel choice over the 'features/ledger as kernel' alternative. Plan trimmed 12→11 pts (old Task 6 lint blocker resolved by ADR 0006). Lesson: when a consolidation ADR is deferred, re-cost the consumer map each review — render sites move (TransactionsCard→LedgerExplorer).

title: Architecture review cadence: always re-grep consumers, don't trust prior ADR's consumer list
content: Process learning for scheduled architecture reviews: an ADR's consumer/call-site map goes stale fast between cycles. The 2026-05-28 ADR 0005 listed ledger/TransactionsCard as the money render site; by 2026-05-29 (v0.7.0) the render had moved to a new LedgerExplorer component and TransactionsCard became a wrapper. Always re-run the grep for each formatter/primitive's actual consumers before re-affirming an ADR, and update the task breakdown's file targets accordingly — handing an engineer a stale path = lost work. Corollary: 'ADR Accepted + tests passing' never means 'adopted' — grep for real consumers of any new primitive; formatMoney has been Accepted (ADR 0004) yet has 0 consumers for two cycles.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-28 23:43 UTC
ADR-0005 money kernel SHIPPED — implement-from-scratch handoff; CategorizePanel is a 4th un-normalized path
content: Team-mode handoff on ai-bookkeeper: Architect re-affirmed ADR-0005 (consolidate money formatting onto src/lib/money kernel + adopt formatMoney app-wide) and handed off the 11-pt breakdown. Git status showed NO code on disk (only the ADR doc addendum + an unrelated README edit) → genuine implement-from-scratch (like ADR-0006), NOT verify+commit. Implemented all 5 tasks directly on main, committed ea3641d (unpushed, review_each_pr human merge gate). Result: src/lib/money/ kernel created (round+arithmetic+format moved out of features/ledger/lib, which now holds export* re-export shims for one release; amount.ts/ingest.ts stayed as ingest-specific); src/lib/format.ts formatCurrency/formatSignedCurrency became thin wrappers over formatMoney(_, 'USD') so all 8 consumers (incl ledger/LedgerExplorer) get drift-free+fail-loud display with ZERO call-site churn; WealthScoreCard inline $…M replaced with kernel formatMoneyCompact; ESLint guardrail (no-restricted-syntax, scoped to features/**/components) bans raw $-templates/toFixed and was verified to fire. Gate: tsc clean, npm test 95/95 (was 87; +8), eslint . clean. KEY DISCOVERY: src/components/CategorizePanel.tsx hand-formats money (`${sign}$${…toLocaleString}`) — a FOURTH un-normalized money path NOT in ADR-0005's consumer map and outside the guardrail's component glob; flagged as a follow-up backlog item (route through kernel + widen guardrail) rather than fixed (out of scope). Implementation pattern that worked: make the shared @/lib/format helpers kernel-backed instead of rewriting 9 components — same drift-free guarantee, signatures stable, minimal risk, matches the ADR's incremental-shipping note.

title: Scope-clean wrapper strategy beats per-component rewrite for formatter consolidation
content: Process learning (ai-bookkeeper, ADR-0005): when consolidating onto a shared formatter, the lowest-risk way to 're-point N consumers' is to make the EXISTING shared helper (src/lib/format.ts formatCurrency/formatSignedCurrency) delegate to the new kernel, rather than editing every component import. The consumers automatically flow through the kernel (drift-free + fail-loud) with zero call-site churn and stable signatures — exactly what the ADR's 'ship incrementally, wrappers keep signatures stable' note recommends. Also: a real ESLint guardrail must be scoped to avoid forcing OUT-OF-SCOPE fixes — a blanket toFixed/`$`-template ban would have flagged formatPercent (src/lib/format.ts), amount.ts error strings, and CategorizePanel.tsx (all outside the accepted item), breaking the eslint release gate. Scoping the rule to src/features/**/components/** caught the render layer being cleaned while leaving out-of-scope files untouched and the gate green. Lesson: design guardrails so `eslint .` stays green after the in-scope fix; flag the unscoped offenders as follow-ups, don't widen scope to satisfy your own rule.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-28 23:57 UTC
ADR-0005 money kernel reviewed APPROVE (ea3641d) — kernel-backed wrappers, gate green, 3 Low notes
content: Reviewed Dev Clone's ADR-0005 implementation commit ea3641d on ai-bookkeeper (consolidate money formatting onto shared src/lib/money kernel). Verdict APPROVE — no security/correctness/critical blockers. Reviewer value-add held: re-ran the gate (tsc clean, npm test 95/95, eslint . clean) and the BUILD-INTEGRITY check PASSED — all src/lib/money siblings (round/arithmetic/format+tests+index) are git-tracked and the 3 features/ledger/lib re-export shims resolve via @/lib/money/* (no untracked-sibling trap this time, unlike ADR-0002's b29eeaa). Also PROVED the new ESLint guardrail actually fires (probe in src/features/**/components tripped both the $${…}-template and .toFixed selectors) — a guardrail that doesn't fire is false security. Correctness property confirmed: formatMoney normalizes via roundMoney before Intl; formatCurrency/formatSignedCurrency are now thin kernel-backed wrappers (zero call-site churn) — the lowest-risk way to realize ADR-0004's display==stored guarantee. 3 LOW notes: (1) format.ts:34-36 formatSignedCurrency derives the sign from the raw pre-rounded value but magnitude from rounded abs → a sub-cent negative rounding to 0 renders '−$0.00', defeating the kernel's −0→0 normalization (unreachable today, ≤2dp at parse boundary); (2) WealthScoreCard compactCurrency + format.ts:130 docstring 'matches prior style' is inaccurate for non-round values — old 1500→$2K / 1e6→$1.0M, Intl now $1.5K / $1M (display-only, arguably better); (3) eslint .toFixed ban covers ALL toFixed in feature components, not just money. ADJACENT out-of-scope (NOT a finding against the change, correctly deferred by implementer): src/components/CategorizePanel.tsx:27 is a 4th un-normalized money path at src/components/, outside ADR-0005's consumer map AND the guardrail's src/features/**/components/** glob — needs its own backlog ticket (route through kernel + widen guardrail glob).

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-29 00:02 UTC
ai-bookkeeper @ ea3641d: postcss CVE STILL OPEN (5th scan); NEW /api/categorize route — unauthenticated+no rate limit (Med), unbounded description (Med); genai unused-dep RESOLVED
content:
Scan at HEAD ea3641d (ADR-0005 money kernel). FINDINGS:

1) postcss XSS GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93 (<8.5.10) STILL OPEN — 5th consecutive scan, overrides block STILL not in package.json. Top-level postcss ^8.5.15 but next@16.2.6 nests postcss@8.4.31 at package-lock.json:3485. npm audit = 2 moderate (postcss + next.via=['postcss'], one root cause). Fix = one-line overrides {"postcss":">=8.5.10"}; npm's next@9.3.3 suggestion is a wrong breaking downgrade. High per known-CVE policy, low real reachability. Next scan: grep package.json for overrides before re-reporting.

2) NEW SURFACE: src/app/api/categorize/route.ts is the FIRST app/api route (prior scans said 'no app/api routes'). It's well-built — GEMINI_API_KEY read server-side only (runtime=nodejs, force-dynamic, no NEXT_PUBLIC, never logged), input validated via parseRequest, SDK dynamically imported. BUT two new findings: M1 — route is UNAUTHENTICATED with NO RATE LIMITING (no middleware.ts) in front of paid Gemini calls = denial-of-wallet + DoS (route.ts:61). M2 — description is UNBOUNDED before buildPrompt (route.ts:22 -> categorize.ts:215) = token-cost amplification + prompt-injection surface; fix = cap length in parseRequest. L1 — model-returned debit/credit/category strings not validated vs chart of accounts (categorize.ts:181-184); prompt-injected reply could surface arbitrary account names (low; human-review suggestions, source forced to 'gemini').

3) RESOLVED: @google/generative-ai (prior Low 'unused dep') is now legitimately imported via dynamic import at route.ts:48 — FIXED, dedupe progress.

INFO/benign: layout.tsx:88 dangerouslySetInnerHTML still static themeInitScript (anti-FOUC). Secrets: 0; only .env.example tracked (empty GEMINI/PLAID/TWILIO placeholders). parseModelJson is solid (shape-validated, confidence clamped, source forced). COVERAGE GAP: Obsidian shared-vault tools unavailable again (continuing).

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-29 00:06 UTC
ai-bookkeeper v0.8.0 SHIPPED @ 5b6a431 — shared money kernel (ADR-0005), MINOR
content: Released v0.8.0 on 2026-05-29 (MINOR, 0.7.0->0.8.0). Triggered by security-scan handoff @ ea3641d. State at run: last tag v0.7.0 (commit 8d43743), HEAD ea3641d with v0.7.0 an ancestor — ONE unreleased feat commit since. git log v0.7.0..HEAD = single feat ea3641d (ADR-0005 money-kernel consolidation): promotes round/arithmetic/format into shared src/lib/money kernel, adopts formatMoney across all 8 render surfaces, adds ESLint guardrail banning raw $${} /toFixed money formatting. Backward-compatible (old features/ledger/lib/{round,arithmetic,format} kept as re-export shims one release) -> MINOR per pre-1.0 policy. KEY: realizes ADR-0004 'display==stored' which previously had 0 consumers; v0.7.0 LedgerExplorer had wired to wrong formatter. Quality gate GREEN on merge result: tsc clean, eslint clean (incl new guardrail), tsx --test 95/95 (NOT 87 — README was stale, corrected 87->95 in 3 spots), next build 8 routes. Increment already on main (no feature branch to merge). Release commit 5b6a431 (bump+CHANGELOG[0.8.0]+docs/releases/v0.8.0.md+README), annotated tag v0.8.0 at 5b6a431, pushed main+tag, published GitHub Release (HTTP 201) on xkazm04/xprize-ai-bookkeeper. CARRY-FORWARD (non-blocking, filed by security persona, not re-triaged): postcss GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93 still open (next@16.2.6 nests postcss@8.4.31; fix=overrides {postcss:>=8.5.10}); /api/categorize unauthenticated+no-rate-limit+unbounded-description; CategorizePanel.tsx 4th un-normalized money path ticketed. LESSON reconfirmed: actual test count (95) came from running tsx --test, not trusting README/commit msg ('95/11' in commit body). Obsidian shared-vault tools still unavailable.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-29 00:13 UTC
ai-bookkeeper v0.8.0: ADR-0005 money kernel shipped — README structure-tree was the only gap (again)
content: v0.8.0 (release 5b6a431) shipped ONE feat: ea3641d ADR-0005 money-kernel consolidation. Money primitives (round/arithmetic/format + tests + barrel) moved into a NEW shared src/lib/money/ kernel; src/features/ledger/lib/{round,arithmetic,format} are now back-compat re-export shims kept ONE release. formatMoney adopted across all 8 render surfaces (incl. LedgerExplorer, WealthScoreCard) → display provably == stored, realizing ADR-0004's previously-unrealized guarantee. New ESLint guardrail bans raw money formatting. Backward-compatible → MINOR. Same release pattern as v0.3.0–v0.7.0: Release Manager pre-authored CHANGELOG [0.8.0] + docs/releases/v0.8.0.md accurately and bumped README test count 87→95; README structure-tree was the ONLY gap. Made 3 doc-only README edits: (1) added money/ to the src/lib/ tree — the recurring HIGHEST-VALUE check, release commits ALWAYS miss new lib dirs; (2) marked ledger/lib round/arithmetic/format as shims→@/lib/money in the enumeration; (3) extended the money-arithmetic Features bullet with the app-wide no-drift framing. KEY: ADR-0005 flipped Proposed→Accepted/implemented — the standing v0.5.0–v0.7.0 caution 'never document ADR-0005 as a feature' is now RETIRED. WATCH: shims are a one-release deprecation window; drop them from the enumeration when a future release deletes them. CategorizePanel.tsx is a 4th un-normalized money path left outside scope (ticketed).
